serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of black coffee brewed from a sachet or filter bag, approximately 240 ml, containing no added sugar or creamer.

nutrient_source.title: Black coffee, brewed from grounds, prepared with tap water — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) taken directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for black coffee (no sugar, sachet/filter bag). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and cholesterol supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 14209 (Coffee, brewed from grounds, prepared with tap water) as HPB does not report these fractions separately.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Black coffee (unsweetened, brewed) is listed in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables with a GI of approximately 29 (low category), referenced to glucose=100. Although the carbohydrate in black coffee (4.4 g/100 ml) is predominantly non-digestible chlorogenic acid conjugates and coffee solids that produce a minimal glycaemic response, the published GI value of 29 is used rather than 0 to reflect the peer-reviewed measurement. Glycemic load per 240 ml serving = (29 × 10.56 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 3.1, rounded to 1.3 per standard 100 ml reference portion used in GI database entry. GL per serving (240 ml): (29 × 10.56) / 100 = 3.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (8 mg/100 ml); no added sugar or cream; moderate caffeine intake within 2–3 cups/day has minimal long-term blood pressure impact per AHA guidelines
hypertension.guidance: Unsweetened black coffee is low in sodium and suitable for individuals with hypertension when consumed in moderation (2–3 cups per day); exceeding 300 mg caffeine per day may cause acute transient blood pressure elevation and should be avoided.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: No added sugar; naturally occurring sugars 1.55 g/100 ml; low GI (29); evidence from ADA-referenced studies shows unsweetened black coffee improves insulin sensitivity and is associated with reduced T2D risk
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened black coffee has a low glycaemic index and does not meaningfully raise blood glucose, making it appropriate for individuals with type 2 diabetes; limit to 2–3 cups per day and avoid adding sugar, flavoured syrups, or creamers which would substantially increase glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat (0.05 g/100 ml), no trans fat, no cholesterol, no added sugar; chlorogenic acids and polyphenols in coffee are associated with reduced cardiovascular risk in moderate consumption
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Unsweetened black coffee contains negligible saturated fat and no cholesterol, and moderate consumption (2–3 cups/day) is associated with reduced cardiovascular risk; individuals with arrhythmia or caffeine sensitivity should limit intake and consult their clinician.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Chlorogenic acids and polyphenols with documented anti-inflammatory properties; no pro-inflammatory components (no added sugar, no saturated fat, no trans fat)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Unsweetened black coffee contains anti-inflammatory polyphenols and is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis in moderation; avoid adding sugar or cream, and limit to 2–3 cups per day if on methotrexate or biologics due to potential caffeine-drug interactions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Black coffee (no sugar, sachet/filter bag) is a brewed beverage prepared by passing hot water through ground coffee contained in a sachet or filter bag, with no sugar, milk, creamer, or flavouring added. The result is a clear, dark brown liquid with a characteristically bitter, slightly acidic flavour and a light body. Per 100 ml, it provides approximately 21 kcal, 0.49 g protein, 0.22 g fat, and 4.4 g carbohydrate (of which 1.55 g is naturally occurring sugar from coffee solids — no added sugar). Sodium is very low at 8 mg/100 ml. The beverage is rich in chlorogenic acids and other polyphenols, which have documented antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It has a low glycaemic index (GI 29) and produces a minimal glycaemic response, making it appropriate for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when consumed without added sugar or cream. Caffeine content is approximately 40–60 mg per 100 ml depending on brew strength. Suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, kosher, gluten-free, and dairy-free diets. Commonly consumed at breakfast or as a mid-morning beverage across Singapore and globally.
common_user_queries: Is black coffee suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in a cup of black coffee?
Is black coffee good for high blood pressure?
Can I drink black coffee if I have heart disease?
What is the GI of black coffee?
Is filter coffee the same as black coffee?
How much sodium is in black coffee?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (confidence 0.95 per input record). Fatty acid fractions and potassium supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 14209 (brewed coffee), which is the closest approved-source equivalent. GI value of 29 (low) sourced from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables (peer-reviewed, Am J Clin Nutr).
notes: calcium_mg is 0.0 and iron_mg is 0.0 as reported by HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for black coffee (no sugar); these are confirmed zero values for a beverage with no dairy or mineral fortification. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — brewed coffee contains no trans fats). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-derived beverage). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-derived beverage). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — no animal-derived or fortified components). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed trace/negligible per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 14209). vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed micronutrient data not reported in HPB Singapore database for this beverage entry; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 14209 reports trace amounts but HPB is the primary source and these values are not confirmed in the primary source. potassium_mg 92.0 mg/100 ml sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 14209 as HPB does not report potassium separately. GI value of 29 (low) used per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 peer-reviewed international GI tables; this supersedes the prior draft value of 0. Glycemic load per 240 ml serving = (29 × 10.56 g) / 100 = 3.1. The per_100g GL reference value is 1.3 (29 × 4.4 / 100). Rheumatoid arthritis suitability set to suitable=true (anti-inflammatory polyphenols present, no pro-inflammatory components).
